title: AI Research Tools
description: Search MAPLE's full database of bills, testimony, and ballot questions by having a conversation with an AI assistant.

section1.title: What is this feature?
section1.desc1: MAPLE now lets you connect AI chat tools—like Claude or ChatGPT—directly to its database. This means you can ask your AI assistant questions about Massachusetts legislation in plain language, and it will search MAPLE's data on bills, ballot questions, and testimony to answer questions or help with research.
section1.desc2Pre: This works through a standard called
section1.desc2Post: , which allows AI tools to securely retrieve live data from services like MAPLE. Think of it as giving your AI assistant a direct line to MAPLE's research library.

section2.title: What can you ask?
section2.intro: Once connected, you can ask your AI assistant to:
section2.item1Main: — search the full text and summaries of all bills across legislative sessions
section2.item2Main: — get a comprehensive view of all bills under a topic, how they relate, and which have the most public support
section2.item3Main: — compare how proposals on a topic have changed across General Court sessions, and identify which ideas have gained or lost traction over time
section2.item4Main: — surface the main arguments, common themes, and organizational voices in testimony on a bill or policy area
section2.item5Main: — see what advocates, organizations, and constituents have said about specific bills
section2.item6Main: — narrow results by committee, primary sponsor, legislation type, or session
section2.item7Main: — find and understand statewide ballot measures alongside legislative bills

section3.title: Example questions to try
section3.intro: Here are some examples of how advocates have used this feature:
section3.example1Text: Find bills about housing affordability that are currently in committee. Summarize what each one proposes.
section3.example2Text: What does H.1234 propose, and what has public testimony said about it? Are there more people who support or oppose it?
section3.example3Text: What bills related to clean water or sewage have been filed in Massachusetts in the last two sessions? Which ones got the most public engagement?
section3.example4Text: I want to testify in favor of expanding paid family leave. What are the relevant bills this session, and what arguments have other supporters already made?

section4.title: How to get started
section4.intro: Follow these steps to connect an AI assistant to MAPLE:
section4.step1Post: . A MAPLE account is required to authenticate your AI assistant's access to the database.
section4.step2Bold: Choose an AI tool that supports MCP.
section4.step2item1Pre: — the easiest option for most users.
section4.step2item2Pre: — available for Pro, Team, and Enterprise subscribers via
section4.step2item2Post: with Developer Mode enabled in settings.
section4.step2item3Main: — MCP is an open standard and support is growing rapidly across AI products. Check your AI tool's documentation for current MCP connection instructions.
section4.step3Post: and click “Get Token” to generate a short-lived access token. Copy it — you'll paste it into your AI tool in the next step.
section4.step4Bold: Add MAPLE as a connected source in your AI tool.
section4.step4Pre: Most tools use a configuration file or settings panel. Add the following connection details, replacing
section4.step5Post: and try one of the example questions above. Your AI assistant will automatically search MAPLE when you ask about Massachusetts legislation.

section5.title: Privacy & data use
section5.desc1: When you use this feature, your AI assistant retrieves only publicly available MAPLE data—the same bills and testimony anyone can read on this site. Your MAPLE token identifies you so that your access is logged, but no private account information is shared with the AI.
section5.desc2Pre: Your conversations are governed by your AI provider's privacy policy, not MAPLE's. We recommend reviewing it before discussing sensitive advocacy work.
section5.desc2LinkText: Learn more about how MAPLE uses AI →
